metadata.title: States PCS: Jharkhand GK for JPSC and other Jharkhand State Exams

questions.question: What is the grant percentage for Primary Food Processing Centers in Jharkhand tribal areas?
questions.explanation: The Jharkhand Food and Feed Processing Policy 2024 was notified in the Jharkhand Gazette on March 18, 2024. It provides a grant of 75% for Primary Food Processing Centers located in tribal areas. This grant applies to maximum eligible project costs, excluding land and working capital. The policy aims to promote food processing activities specifically among tribal and forest dwellers in Jharkhand.

questions.question: What is Jharkhand’s state subsidy for beekeeping under ‘Mithi Kranti’?
questions.explanation: Jharkhand’s ‘Sweet Revolution’ (Mithi Kranti) provided ₹80,000 subsidy per beekeeping unit. Each unit cost ₹1,00,000, with the farmer contributing ₹20,000. The subsidy covered 20 bee colonies, 20 honey boxes, and a honey extractor. The first phase allocated ₹10 crore for 1,207 beneficiaries via the Chief Minister’s Bee Board. Training was provided by Birsa Agricultural University and Krishi Vigyan Kendras. The scheme is now part of the National Beekeeping & Honey Mission extended to FY 2025-26.

questions.question: Which body implements PMGSY in Jharkhand?

options.label: A
options.text: Jharkhand State Road and Bridge Development Authority

options.label: B
options.text: Gram and Gram Panchayat Road Development Authority

options.label: C
options.text: Jharkhand State Rural Roads Development Authority

answer.label: C
answer.text: Jharkhand State Rural Roads Development Authority

questions.explanation: The Jharkhand State Rural Roads Development Authority (JSRRDA) implements PMGSY in Jharkhand. PMGSY is a central government initiative managed at the state level by agencies like JSRRDA. This agency oversees funding, audits, banking, and agreements related to PMGSY projects in Jharkhand. Other authorities in the state do not have the jurisdiction for PMGSY implementation. PMGSY aims to improve rural road connectivity across India, with state-level agencies handling execution.

questions.question: Which state first implemented Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) for kerosene subsidy?
questions.explanation: Jharkhand was the first state to implement DBT for kerosene subsidy starting October 1, 2016. The DBT scheme was launched in districts including Chatra, Hazaribagh, Khunti, and Jamtara. Under this scheme, kerosene was sold at market price and the subsidy credited directly to Aadhaar-linked bank accounts. This initiative aimed to reduce leakages in the Public Distribution System. The national pilot for DBT in kerosene subsidy began in multiple states from April 1, 2016.

questions.question: What is the full form of JTELP, an IFAD project completed in 2021?

options.label: A
options.text: Jharkhand Tribal Empowerment and Livelihood Programme

options.label: B
options.text: Jharkhand Tribal Education and Livelihood Programme

options.label: C
options.text: Jharkhand Teachers Empowerment and Livelihood Programme

options.label: D
options.text: Jharkhand Teachers Education and Learning Programme

answer.label: A
answer.text: Jharkhand Tribal Empowerment and Livelihood Programme

questions.explanation: JTELP stands for Jharkhand Tribal Empowerment and Livelihood Programme. It was implemented by Jharkhand Tribal Development Society under the Welfare Department. The project targeted 136,000 tribal households in 14 Tribal Sub-Plan districts, later expanded to 211,000 households. JTELP was approved in 2012, started in 2013, and completed in December 2021 with loan closure in June 2022. The project focused on community empowerment, natural resource management, and livelihoods for tribal groups, including Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups (PVTGs).

questions.question: Who authored ‘Singhbhum, Seraikela-Kharsawan Through The Ages’?
questions.explanation: Tikait Nrapendra Narayan Singh wrote the book ‘Singhbhum, Seraikela-Kharsawan Through The Ages’ published circa 1955 in Calcutta. Bibliographic records list him as the author. Other individuals named Tikait Umrao Singh, Jagannath Singh, and Kunal Pratap Singh have no documented association with this work. The book covers historical aspects of Singhbhum, Seraikela, and Kharsawan regions. It is distinct from L.S.S. O’Malley’s 1910 Bengal District Gazetteer which addresses the same areas but is a different publication.

questions.question: Jhuma Khatoon is associated with which sport?
questions.explanation: Jhuma Khatoon is an Indian athlete specializing in middle and long-distance running events like 800m, 1500m, and 5000m. She holds national youth records for West Bengal in the 800m and 1500m races. She won a silver medal at the Asian Championships and competed in the Asian Games. There is no record linking her to hockey, boxing, or cycling.

questions.question: Which sport is Pramod Kumar Bedia associated with?
questions.explanation: Pramod Kumar Bedia is a Wushu athlete from Jharkhand. He competed in the 60kg category and participated in several Jharkhand State Sub Junior Wushu Championships. His name appears on the Wushu Jharkhand website as a player. He has won a silver medal at the 11th Jharkhand State Sub Junior Wushu Championship. He is listed among Indian squad Wushu players alongside others from Jharkhand.

questions.question: Where were the 1928 Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The 1928 Summer Olympics, called the IX Olympiad, took place in Amsterdam, Netherlands, from July 28 to August 12, 1928. Amsterdam was selected after bidding against Los Angeles. The Games included 2,883 athletes from 46 countries competing in 109 events across 14 sports. The 1928 Olympics introduced the Olympic flame and women’s athletics. Germany returned to compete post-World War I.

questions.question: Which two Jain Tirthankars are mentioned in the Rigveda?
questions.explanation: Rishabhdev is the 1st Tirthankara and Arishtnemi (Neminath) is the 22nd Tirthankara in Jainism. Both are explicitly mentioned in the Rigveda. Ajitnath, the 2nd Tirthankara, is mentioned in the Yajurveda but not in the Rigveda. The Rigveda is one of the oldest sacred texts of India, composed around 1500–1200 BCE.
